nodes.name: Anthropic Skills
nodes.description: Public repository for Agent Skills - official Claude Code skills from Anthropic
nodes.why: Official Anthropic skills repo for Claude Code agent, rapidly growing as the standard for agent skill definitions

nodes.name: Hermes Agent
nodes.description: The agent that grows with you - AI agent framework from Nous Research
nodes.why: One of the fastest-growing agent frameworks, focusing on adaptive learning and personalization

nodes.name: DeerFlow
nodes.description: Open-source long-horizon SuperAgent harness with sandboxes, memories, tools, skills
nodes.why: ByteDance's #1 GitHub Trending project. Super agent harness with sub-agents and memory

nodes.name: OpenManus
nodes.description: Open-source framework for building general AI agents
nodes.why: Built by MetaGPT community. Viral open-source agent framework with massive community adoption

nodes.name: MiroFish
nodes.description: Simple and Universal Swarm Intelligence Engine, Predicting Anything
nodes.why: Gained 2,782 stars in a single day. Novel bio-inspired swarm intelligence approach

nodes.name: CUA
nodes.description: Open-source infrastructure for Computer-Use Agents with sandboxed desktops
nodes.why: Critical infrastructure for training/evaluating GUI agents on full desktops

nodes.name: OpenClaw
nodes.description: Your own personal AI assistant with 50+ integrations, any OS, any platform
nodes.why: Fastest-growing open-source project in GitHub history. Fully local personal AI assistant

nodes.name: n8n
nodes.description: Open-source workflow automation with native AI capabilities, 400+ integrations
nodes.why: The go-to platform for AI-driven workflow automation and multi-system agent flows

nodes.name: AutoResearch
nodes.description: AI agents running research on single-GPU nanochat training automatically
nodes.why: Andrej Karpathy's research agent project. Automates ML research on single GPU

nodes.name: GenericAgent
nodes.description: Self-evolving agent that grows skill tree from 3.3K-line seed
nodes.why: Novel self-evolving agent architecture that grows its own capabilities with 6x less token consumption

nodes.name: OpenSRE
nodes.description: Build your own AI SRE agents. Open source toolkit for the AI era
nodes.why: DevOps/SRE automation via AI agents is an emerging high-value use case

nodes.name: Browser-Use Web UI
nodes.description: Run AI agents directly in the browser without installation
nodes.why: Removes installation friction for autonomous web agents

nodes.name: Free Claude Code
nodes.description: Use claude-code for free in the terminal, VSCode extension or via discord
nodes.why: Democratizes access to Claude Code functionality for developers

nodes.name: Awesome Codex Skills
nodes.description: Curated list of practical Codex skills for automating workflows
nodes.why: Growing rapidly as developers seek to extend AI coding agents with practical skills

nodes.name: Awesome Claude Skills
nodes.description: Curated list of practical Claude Skills with 500+ app integrations via Composio
nodes.why: Massive collection of skills for Claude Code with real app automation across 1000+ services

nodes.name: Matt Pocock Skills
nodes.description: Agent Skills for real engineers. Straight from my .claude directory
nodes.why: Popular TypeScript educator sharing production Claude skills

nodes.name: Karpathy Skills
nodes.description: A single CLAUDE.md file to improve Claude Code behavior, derived from Karpathy's observations
nodes.why: Andrej Karpathy's insights on LLM coding translated into Claude skills. Gained 30k stars in a week

nodes.name: Spec Kit
nodes.description: Toolkit to help you get started with Spec-Driven Development
nodes.why: GitHub's official spec-driven development toolkit for AI-assisted coding workflows

nodes.name: GitNexus
nodes.description: Zero-Server Code Intelligence Engine with knowledge graph and GraphRAG Agent
nodes.why: Browser-based code exploration with knowledge graphs - no server needed

nodes.name: CodeAssist
nodes.description: Completely private and local AI coding assistant that learns from your keystrokes
nodes.why: Learns from every keystroke to adapt to your coding style. Privacy-first local assistant

nodes.name: Claude Code Templates
nodes.description: CLI tool for configuring and monitoring Claude Code with templates and dashboards
nodes.why: Practical templates for Claude Code with monitoring capabilities for team usage

nodes.name: Ollama
nodes.description: Get up and running with LLMs locally
nodes.why: Solved the last mile problem for local LLMs. Made local AI accessible to non-technical users

nodes.name: Thunderbolt
nodes.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in
nodes.why: Thunderbird's entry into AI with focus on privacy and local model control

nodes.name: InstructRAG
nodes.description: ICLR 2025 - Instructing RAG via Self-Synthesized Rationales
nodes.why: Up to 8.3% better results across benchmarks. Self-synthesized rationales for RAG denoising

nodes.name: Awesome Local LLM
nodes.description: Complete guide to running powerful AI models locally in 2025
nodes.why: Comprehensive resource for the exploding local LLM movement

nodes.name: RLM
nodes.description: General plug-and-play inference library for Recursive Language Models with sandbox support
nodes.why: Novel Recursive Language Models with sandbox support for structured generation

nodes.name: Stable Diffusion WebUI
nodes.description: Stable Diffusion web UI - browser interface for image generation
nodes.why: Most popular interface for diffusion image generation. Cultural phenomenon

nodes.name: DiffusionDrive
nodes.description: CVPR 2025 Highlight - Truncated Diffusion Model for Real-Time End-to-End Autonomous Driving
nodes.why: 10x faster diffusion for autonomous driving with record-breaking 88.1 PDMS on NAVSIM

nodes.name: DiG
nodes.description: CVPR 2025 - Scalable and Efficient Diffusion Models with Gated Linear Attention
nodes.why: 2.5x faster than DiT with 75.7% GPU memory savings at high resolution

nodes.name: StreamingT2V
nodes.description: CVPR 2025 - Consistent, Dynamic, and Extendable Long Video Generation from Text
nodes.why: Generates long high-quality videos up to 2 minutes with temporal consistency

nodes.name: ManiFlow Policy
nodes.description: CoRL 2025 - General Robot Manipulation Policy via Consistency Flow Training
nodes.why: 98.3% improvement on real-world tasks. Single policy works across humanoid, bimanual, and single-arm robots

nodes.name: YOTO
nodes.description: RSS 2025 - You Only Teach Once: One-Shot Bimanual Robotic Manipulation from Video
nodes.why: Novel approach to teach bimanual manipulation from single video demonstrations

nodes.name: Robots Pre-Train Robots
nodes.description: ICLR 2025 - Manipulation-Centric Robotic Representation from Large-Scale Robot Datasets
nodes.why: Pre-training robot representations from large-scale datasets for better manipulation

nodes.name: Robust Gymnasium
nodes.description: ICLR 2025 - Unified Modular Benchmark for Robust Reinforcement Learning
nodes.why: Comprehensive benchmark for robust RL with perturbed observations, actions, rewards, dynamics

nodes.name: MultiTalk
nodes.description: NeurIPS 2025 - Audio-Driven Multi-Person Conversational Video Generation
nodes.why: Generates multi-person conversation, singing, and interaction videos from audio input

nodes.name: V2PE
nodes.description: ICCV 2025 - Variable Visual Position Encoding for Multimodal Long-Context VLMs
nodes.why: Enables VLMs to handle sequences up to 1M tokens with novel positional encoding

nodes.name: MMRL
nodes.description: CVPR 2025 - Multi-Modal Representation Learning for Vision-Language Models
nodes.why: Parameter-efficient representation learning with strong generalization for VLMs

nodes.name: VaLiK
nodes.description: ICCV 2025 - Annotation-Free Multimodal Knowledge Graph Construction for LLMs
nodes.why: Zero-shot multimodal classification and QA by constructing knowledge graphs without annotations

nodes.name: Claude Context
nodes.description: Code search MCP for Claude Code. Make entire codebase the context for coding agents
nodes.why: Solves the critical context limitation problem using Milvus vector search

nodes.name: Beads
nodes.description: Memory upgrade for your coding agent - persistent agent memory
nodes.why: Addresses the critical need for persistent agent memory across coding sessions

nodes.name: Context Mode
nodes.description: Context window optimization for AI coding agents, 98% reduction, 14 platforms
nodes.why: Solves token bloat problem for coding agents across multiple platforms

nodes.name: MCP Go
nodes.description: Go implementation of Model Context Protocol for LLM integration with external data
nodes.why: Standard Go implementation of Anthropic's MCP protocol for LLM tool integration

nodes.name: Model Context Protocol
nodes.description: Specification and documentation for Model Context Protocol - standard for LLM tool/data integration
nodes.why: Official MCP spec from Anthropic. Becoming the de facto standard for LLM context and tool access

nodes.name: MCP Solver
nodes.description: MCP server for constraint optimization and solving - SAT, SMT, ASP capabilities
nodes.why: Novel MCP server bridging symbolic solvers with LLMs. Published at SAT 2025

nodes.name: Fincept Terminal
nodes.description: AI-powered financial terminal with market analytics and economic data tools
nodes.why: AI-powered financial terminal for data-driven decision making

nodes.name: TS-RAG
nodes.description: NeurIPS 2025 - RAG based Time Series Foundation Models for Zero-Shot Forecasting
nodes.why: Novel RAG framework for time series forecasting with strong zero-shot generalization

nodes.name: TrendRadar
nodes.description: AI-driven public opinion and trend monitor with multi-platform aggregation and smart alerts
nodes.why: AI-powered multi-platform trend monitoring for market research and social listening

nodes.name: Langflow
nodes.description: Low-code platform for designing and deploying AI-powered agents and RAG workflows
nodes.why: Democratizes creation of complex AI agent pipelines without extensive coding

nodes.name: LlamaIndex
nodes.description: Data framework for LLM applications with 300+ integrations for data connectors
nodes.why: Major data framework for RAG and LLM apps. 300+ integrations for connecting LLMs to private data

nodes.name: LangChain
nodes.description: Framework for developing applications powered by language models
nodes.why: Created the vocabulary for LLM apps: chains, agents, RAG, tool use. Influenced entire generation

nodes.name: RealtimeSTT
nodes.description: Robust, efficient, low-latency speech-to-text library with VAD and wake word
nodes.why: Industry-standard real-time speech recognition using Faster-Whisper and SileroVAD

nodes.name: AI Coding Landscape
nodes.description: Comprehensive landscape of AI coding models, agents, CLIs, IDEs, benchmarks
nodes.why: Essential reference tracking the rapidly evolving AI coding ecosystem

nodes.name: OpenCode
nodes.description: Fastest-growing open-source coding agent with 150k+ stars. Full IDE replacement
nodes.why: Leading open alternative to Claude Code/Cursor with local and cloud model support

nodes.name: Claude Mem
nodes.description: Solves context-loss in coding agents by capturing and retrieving session history
nodes.why: One of the most impactful agent-memory projects for Claude Code

nodes.name: Learn Claude Code
nodes.description: Educational nano agent harness built from 0 to 1 in bash/TypeScript
nodes.why: Massive demand for understanding how agentic coding tools work under the hood

nodes.name: Crush
nodes.description: Beautiful terminal UI for agentic coding from makers of Charm CLI tools
nodes.why: Demonstrates developer experience matters enormously in coding agents

nodes.name: Serena
nodes.description: MCP toolkit providing semantic code retrieval and editing as an IDE for agents
nodes.why: Enables deep codebase understanding that most agents lack

nodes.name: KiloCode
nodes.description: All-in-one agentic engineering platform, superset of Roo, Cline, and custom features
nodes.why: Consolidating multiple open-source coding assistant paradigms

nodes.name: Gemini CLI
nodes.description: Google's official open-source Gemini CLI with multimodal and voice mode
nodes.why: Brings multimodal Gemini directly to the terminal with built-in memory systems

nodes.name: Paperclip
nodes.description: Open-source orchestration for zero-human companies via multi-agent coordination
nodes.why: Pioneers fully automated business operations through autonomous workflows

nodes.name: Oh My OpenAgent
nodes.description: Lightweight agent harness for IDE integration focusing on speed and minimal resources
nodes.why: Alternative to heavy agent frameworks with broad IDE compatibility

nodes.name: WorldMonitor
nodes.description: Real-time global intelligence dashboard combining AI news with geopolitical monitoring
nodes.why: Combines AI news aggregation with geopolitical and infrastructure monitoring

nodes.name: Swarm
nodes.description: OpenAI's educational multi-agent orchestration framework
nodes.why: Architectural blueprint for countless agent systems worldwide despite being educational

nodes.name: Agent-S
nodes.description: Open agentic framework using computers through vision and GUI manipulation
nodes.why: Key project for computer-use agents interacting with standard desktop environments

nodes.name: kagent
nodes.description: Cloud-native agentic AI platform built for Kubernetes with MCP support
nodes.why: CNCF-aligned platform for enterprise DevOps and infrastructure automation

nodes.name: nanoChat
nodes.description: The best ChatGPT that $100 can buy. Minimal hardware conversational AI
nodes.why: Demonstrates high-quality conversational AI can run on minimal hardware

nodes.name: LangExtract
nodes.description: Google's library for extracting structured information from text using LLMs
nodes.why: Critical for RAG pipelines and knowledge extraction with precise source grounding

nodes.name: VeRL
nodes.description: ByteDance's production-ready RL post-training framework for LLMs
nodes.why: Trained Doubao-1.5-pro and Seed-Thinking models with GRPO, PPO, DAPO

nodes.name: AI Agents for Beginners
nodes.description: Microsoft's official 12-lesson course for building AI agents
nodes.why: Most popular educational resource for agent development with RAG and agentic workflow content

nodes.name: ADK Python
nodes.description: Google's official Agent Development Kit for Python
nodes.why: Code-first framework with multi-agent hierarchies, tracing, evaluation, deployment

nodes.name: ADK Go
nodes.description: Google's ADK for Go leveraging concurrency for high-performance agents
nodes.why: Idiomatic Go agent toolkit for cloud-native agent applications at enterprise scale

nodes.name: Spring AI Alibaba
nodes.description: Agentic AI framework for Java developers with Spring integration
nodes.why: Brings agent capabilities to the massive Java enterprise ecosystem

nodes.name: MemPalace
nodes.description: Best-benchmarked open-source AI memory system with ChromaDB and MCP support
nodes.why: Best-in-class memory layer for agents solving state persistence

nodes.name: Graphiti
nodes.description: Real-time knowledge graphs for AI agents from streaming data
nodes.why: Constructs dynamic knowledge graphs with MCP integration for agent long-term reasoning

nodes.name: VibeVoice
nodes.description: Microsoft's open-source frontier voice AI for speech synthesis and interaction
nodes.why: Pushes open audio AI to commercial-grade quality for conversational AI

nodes.name: Chatterbox
nodes.description: State-of-the-art open-source TTS rivaling commercial solutions
nodes.why: High-quality text-to-speech pushing open audio AI to production quality

nodes.name: Context7
nodes.description: Documentation platform for LLMs and AI editors as MCP server
nodes.why: Solves stale-documentation problem in vibe-coding with contextual docs

nodes.name: FastMCP
nodes.description: Fast, Pythonic MCP framework for building Model Context Protocol servers
nodes.why: De facto standard for tool-calling infrastructure in agent ecosystems

nodes.name: AWS MCP
nodes.description: AWS official MCP servers for CloudWatch, Lambda, S3 in agent workflows
nodes.why: Brings AWS best practices directly into AI agent workflows through MCP

nodes.name: ToolHive
nodes.description: Enterprise-grade MCP server manager for Kubernetes
nodes.why: Secures and orchestrates MCP servers in production with access control

nodes.name: RoboBrain
nodes.description: CVPR 2025 robotics brain model with abstract-to-concrete reasoning
nodes.why: Unified model for robotic manipulation, highly influential in embodied AI research

nodes.name: TensorFlow
nodes.description: An Open Source Machine Learning Framework for Everyone
nodes.why: The framework that democratized deep learning. Powered countless production systems

nodes.name: PyTorch
nodes.description: Tensors and Dynamic neural networks in Python with strong GPU acceleration
nodes.why: Dynamic computation graphs made debugging easier. De facto standard for deep learning research

nodes.name: Transformers
nodes.description: State-of-the-art ML models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal
nodes.why: Central nervous system of modern AI. Made thousands of pretrained models accessible

nodes.name: AutoGPT
nodes.description: Build, Deploy, and Run AI Agents
nodes.why: The repo that started the agent revolution. Proved LLMs could be autonomous actors

nodes.name: scikit-learn
nodes.description: Machine learning in Python
nodes.why: Bedrock of classical ML in Python. Clean API design became the standard

nodes.name: Keras
nodes.description: Deep Learning for humans
nodes.why: Proved deep learning APIs could be intuitive. Primary way millions first experienced deep learning

nodes.name: vLLM
nodes.description: High-throughput and memory-efficient inference engine for LLMs
nodes.why: Introduced PagedAttention. Became the production standard for LLM inference

nodes.name: LLaMA
nodes.description: Inference code for Llama models
nodes.why: Open-weight LLM that changed the industry. Spawned Alpaca, Vicuna, entire open-weights ecosystem

nodes.name: Whisper
nodes.description: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision
nodes.why: Democratized speech recognition across 99 languages. Default ASR for open-source apps

nodes.name: CLIP
nodes.description: Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining
nodes.why: Connected vision and language, unlocking the generative AI revolution. Conditioning for Stable Diffusion

nodes.name: nanoGPT
nodes.description: The simplest, fastest repository for training/finetuning medium-sized GPTs
nodes.why: Stripped GPT training to essentials. Standard teaching tool for transformer training

nodes.name: Detectron2
nodes.description: Object detection, segmentation and visual recognition platform
nodes.why: Standardized detection and segmentation research with modular design and production deployment

nodes.name: OpenAI Gym
nodes.description: A toolkit for developing and comparing reinforcement learning algorithms
nodes.why: Created the standard API for RL environments. Foundation of modern RL research

nodes.name: Diffusers
nodes.description: State-of-the-art diffusion models for image, video, and audio generation
nodes.why: Unified API for diffusion models. Made Stable Diffusion and ControlNet accessible

nodes.name: LLaVA
nodes.description: Visual Instruction Tuning (NeurIPS'23 Oral)
nodes.why: Demonstrated simple CLIP+LLM architecture could achieve GPT-4V-level multimodal capabilities

nodes.name: DeepSpeed
nodes.description: Deep learning optimization library for distributed training and inference
nodes.why: Made training billion-parameter models feasible. ZeRO optimizer eliminated memory redundancies

nodes.name: GPT4All
nodes.description: Local LLM for consumer-grade hardware
nodes.why: Proved capable LLMs could run on consumer laptops. Contributed to local/edge AI movement

nodes.name: JAX
nodes.description: Composable transformations of NumPy programs
nodes.why: Powers AlphaFold and Gemini training. Next-generation ML framework with XLA compilation

nodes.name: Continue
nodes.description: Open-source AI coding assistant for VS Code and JetBrains
nodes.why: Open-source challenge to Copilot. Allows full customization of models, prompts, context

nodes.name: Stanford Alpaca
nodes.description: Code and data to train Stanford's Alpaca models
nodes.why: Showed instruction-following via $600 GPT-3.5 distillation. Kicked off open-instruction-tuning race

nodes.name: MLflow
nodes.description: Open source AI engineering platform for lifecycle management
nodes.why: Standard for ML lifecycle management. Solved reproducibility crisis with versioning

nodes.name: Datasets
nodes.description: The largest hub of ready-to-use datasets for AI models
nodes.why: Standardized data access with 650+ datasets. Arrow-based backend for massive datasets

nodes.name: TorchVision
nodes.description: Datasets, Transforms and Models for Computer Vision
nodes.why: Standardized CV datasets and pretrained models. Default starting point for vision projects

nodes.name: OpenAI Baselines
nodes.description: High-quality implementations of reinforcement learning algorithms
nodes.why: Reference implementations of A2C, PPO, DQN. Default algorithm for robotics agents

nodes.name: Stable Diffusion
nodes.description: A latent text-to-image diffusion model
nodes.why: Original release that made high-quality image generation accessible on consumer GPUs

nodes.name: CrewAI
nodes.description: Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents
nodes.why: Pioneered multi-agent orchestration with role-based approach. Agent teams outperform single agents

nodes.name: minGPT
nodes.description: A minimal PyTorch re-implementation of GPT
nodes.why: At ~300 lines, proved transformers were clean, understandable algorithms. Most referenced educational resource

nodes.name: GPT-Neo
nodes.description: An implementation of GPT-2/3-like models by EleutherAI
nodes.why: First serious open-source attempt to replicate GPT-3. Laid groundwork for open LLM movement

nodes.name: BLOOM
nodes.description: Open multilingual LLM by BigScience (176B parameters, 46 languages)
nodes.why: First truly open multilingual LLM. Demonstrated open science could produce competitive models

nodes.name: char-rnn
nodes.description: Multi-layer RNN/LSTM/GRU for character-level language models
nodes.why: The hello world of deep learning for text generation. Inspired the modern language modeling era

nodes.name: Weights & Biases
nodes.description: The AI developer platform for training and fine-tuning models
nodes.why: Solved experiment tracking for deep learning. Clean dashboards became the standard for ML teams

nodes.name: Jukebox
nodes.description: A Generative Model for Music
nodes.why: Generated raw audio waveforms of music with singing. Proved AI music generation at studio quality
